symptoms.onset: escalating over two weeks, worst tonight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue is a common non-specific symptom of hyperglycaemia and poor sleep from nocturia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturia four to five times per night (polyuria)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic diuresis from glycosuria is the mechanism of the polyuria/nocturia.
Isolated frequency without dysuria/haematuria favours osmotic diuresis over infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Beefy-red weepy rash confined to the right inguinal fold with satellite lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Satellite lesions on a macerated flexural erythema are characteristic of cutaneous candidiasis.
Confinement to the fold with satellite spots and no streaking argues against spreading bacterial infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dark, velvety, thickened flexural skin (acanthosis nigricans) with skin tags

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acanthosis nigricans and acrochordons are cutaneous markers of hyperinsulinaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central abdominal obesity with increasing waist circumference

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central adiposity is a core driver of insulin resistance and a defining metabolic-syndrome criterion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strong family history of type 2 diabetes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: First-degree family history substantially raises pre-test probability of impaired glucose regulation.

investigation_details.expected_result: Borderline-high random reading (~9.8 mmol/L) — suggestive but sub-diagnostic for diabetes; below the 11.1 mmol/L random threshold
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Reassuring but non-diagnostic; a fasting venous sample is still required regardless of this reading.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with hyperglycaemia; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified and not diagnostic on its own.
Cannot confirm or exclude frank diabetes; fasting venous plasma glucose + HbA1c required.
Hypertension is a metabolic-syndrome criterion; here it is a sealed clinician measurement — no validated home cuff reading was offered, so the AI cannot obtain it remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dysuria, haematuria or suprapubic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of urinary-tract-irritative features makes UTI unlikely and points to osmotic diuresis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No systemic infective or hyperglycaemic-emergency features (afebrile, no vomiting, no Kussmaul breathing, alert, tolerating fluids)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative emergency screen keeps this a lower-acuity outpatient presentation.
Afebrile and systemically well argues against sepsis at this time.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acanthosis nigricans, central obesity, family history and hypertension together strongly indicate insulin resistance and warrant formal glycaemic testing.
Fatigue, osmotic polyuria, glycosuria-driven candidiasis and a raised random glucose form a coherent symptomatic-hyperglycaemia picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to categorise glycaemic status; distinguishes pre-diabetes from frank diabetes. Arrange for the next fasting morning.
Confirms average glycaemia over ~3 months and supports diagnosis/monitoring; complements fasting glucose.
Completes cardiometabolic risk assessment in suspected metabolic syndrome.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urinalysis (dipstick for glucose; MSU only if urinary symptoms)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Demonstrates glycosuria explaining the polyuria; routine culture not needed given no dysuria/haematuria.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin swab of the groin rash for microscopy/culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not first-line — the appearance is clinically candidal; reserve swabbing for treatment failure or suspected bacterial superinfection.
